repos.name: btcpayserver
repos.description: Accept Bitcoin payments. Free, open-source & self-hosted, Bitcoin payment processor.

repos.name: directory.btcpayserver.org
repos.description: BTCPay Server Directory - Merchants, Projects & Charities accepting Bitcoin

repos.name: btcpayserver-plugin-builder
repos.description: A server building plugins for BTCPay Server

repos.name: btcpayserver-translator
repos.description: A command-line tool to translate BTCPay Server's UI text to multiple languages using OpenRouter AI service. Now enjoy BTCPay Server in your local languages.

issues.title: New entry submission - SUP Norway
issues.body:
New submission:

Name: SUP Norway
Url: https://supnorway.com/
Type: merchants
SubType: holiday-travel
Description: Unique and exclusive stand up paddling adventures in the most magnificent nature Norway has to offer.
Top line equipment, the most experienced guides, on the most beautiful UNESCO
Insta: https://instagram.com/supnorway/
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/c/supnorway
Twitter: @supnorway

issues.title: Poor contrast on Listing Requests count badge in dark mode
issues.body: In dark mode, the request count badge next to Listing Requests is hard to read. The badge uses a yellow/orange background with a very light number color, which creates low contrast and makes the number difficult to see.

issues.title: [Feature] Add an Unlisted indicator for plugins in public directory search results
issues.body:
Currently, when a user searches the public plugin directory, listed and unlisted plugins appear visually identical in the results.

Example scenario:

A user searches for a plugin author in the public directory.
The results include both listed and unlisted plugins by that author.
All plugin entries look the same.
The user cannot tell which plugins are unlisted.
Expected behavior:

Unlisted plugins should have a clear visual indicator in the public directory UI, such as an Unlisted badge/flag next to the plugin name or status area.

This would make it clear to users that the plugin is available

issues.title: [Bug]  Admin primary owner list changes with pagination
issues.body:
## Description 

On /admin/plugins, the Email Primary Owners button generates a different email list depending on the current table page. It should include primary owners from all plugins matching the current filters, not just the items shown on the current page.

issues.title: Create a dedicated `Plugins` page dedicated to user docs
issues.body:
Currently, the user documentation lists many plugins in the sidebar, but there is no dedicated Plugins page that serves as a proper introduction for end users.

We do have a developer-focused page at https://docs.btcpayserver.org/Development/Plugins/, but regular BTCPay Server users are not clearly introduced to plugins from a user perspective.

The goal of this issue is to create a clear first introduction point for plugins in the user documentation, so users better understand what plugins are, how they become available, and how they can expand BTCPay Server functionality.

A dedicated user-f

issues.title: Update `WhatsNext`page for core plugin terminology changes
issues.body:
The https://docs.btcpayserver.org/WhatsNext/ page needs to be updated to reflect the removal of the distinction between Apps and Plugins.

As explained in issue #1559, BTCPay Server no longer separates Apps and Plugins. What used to be called apps are now core system plugins. From the user’s perspective, features such as Point of Sale and Crowdfunding are now presented as plugins in the sidebar.
T
he goal of this task is straightforward: remove references to apps and make sure the page consistently refers to Crowdfunding, Point of Sale, and Pay Button as plugins, within the plugins section.

T

issues.title: Refactor walkthrough.md - remove mentions of apps and unify plugins terminology
issues.body:
Our documentation at https://docs.btcpayserver.org/Walkthrough is slightly outdated.

The goal of that page is to walk users through each item in the sidebar in the order it appears, give a short explanation of what it does, and link to a dedicated page where they can learn more about that feature.
Here’s the proposed approach for refactoring this page:

1. Set up a local BTCPay Server instance if you do not already have access to a server with admin permissions. This is important because some sidebar features are only visible to admins. We should also highlight this in the documentation, sinc

issues.title: Standardize Wallet View: Add Date Filter for Transactions
issues.body: **Context:** As mentioned in [Issue #3954](https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/issues/3954), there is a need to standardize the Wallet view so that it offers feature parity with other views in BTCPay Server. **Request:** Enhance the Wallet transaction view to allow users to filter transactions by date in the same way as the Payment Requests and Invoices views. This would help users more easily locate relevant transactions and improve consistency across

issues.title: Remove Wabisabi Plugin documentation
issues.body:
This page refers to a plugin not available anymore: https://docs.btcpayserver.org/Wabisabi

Are there any plans to bring the plugin back or should this page better be removed?

issues.title: Add Support for More Languages in BTCPay Server Translator
issues.body:
### Description

The BTCPay Server Translator currently supports several key languages, but we’re looking to **expand support for more widely used global languages** to make BTCPay Server accessible to even more users.

This issue tracks adding **new major languages** to the translation tool and updating relevant configuration, documentation, and testing.

---

###  Currently Supported Languages

The following translations are already available:

- 🇫🇷 French (`french.json`)
- 🇮🇳 Hindi (`hindi.json`)
- 🇯🇵 Japanese (`japanese.json`)
- 🇧🇷 Portuguese (Brazil) (`portuguese (brazil).json`)
-

issues.title: Poor `Pending` status contrast in Light mode > Withdraw > Pending status
issues.body: Good first issue for new devs wanting to contribute, pretty straightforward.

issues.title: Startup Icon Appears Low Resolution and Pixelated
issues.body:
The app's startup/splash screen icon appears in low resolution and pixelated when launching the app. 

It may not be visible from the screenshot, but if  you deploy on the device, I am sure you can clearly tell.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/105abca3-28fe-4076-928a-cb1e121ab688)

testerItems.title: Allows merchants configure tax inclusion or exclusion
testerItems.body: Part of pending tax issues as stated here https://github.com/btcpayserver/btcpayserver/issues/6713 This PR aims to resolve "Tax included or tax excluded price in keypad (allowing merchants to configure if the amount they apply in keypad includes tax or not)"

testerItems.title: Refactor navigation: topbar global actions and unified search
testerItems.body:
This PR is a prototype for #6902 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/43281d64-b667-448b-ade9-29a0648a8288

It adds:

- Global search (local nav index + server-backexd results).
- Global top-right actions (notifications/account/server operations moved from sidebar context).
- Navigation restructuring to keep store-focused items in the sidebar and global actions in the top bar.
- A dismissible migration hint in the sidebar to guide users to the new top-right location for server/account navigation.
- Mobile navigation adjustments, including moving store selection into the hambu

testerItems.title: feat(wallets): add transaction search and date filters.
testerItems.body:
## Summary
Adds invoice‑style search and date filtering to the wallet transactions view, so users can quickly locate transactions by TXID or time range. The UI mirrors the invoice/payment request patterns and filters apply consistently to infinite scroll and exports.

## Problem
Per #7091 and #7092 (and #3954), wallet transactions lacked:
- A search bar for transaction IDs (and related metadata)
- Date filtering controls consistent with invoices/payment requests
- Combined filter support when paging or exporting

This made it harder to locate specific transactions and created UX incon

testerItems.title: UI: clarify LND seed recovery expectations
testerItems.body:
Fixes #5612.

Clarifies the LND Seed Backup page copy to set correct expectations for recovery steps under some deployments (e.g. BTCPay Server Docker), where LND may be initialized/unlocked automatically and recovery may require an interactive shell (TTY) and temporarily disabling automatic init/unlock.

Changes:
- Add a short clarification paragraph on the LND Seed Backup page.
- Add the new string to Translations.Default.cs for localization support.

How to verify:
- Server → Services → LND Seed Backup: confirm the new paragraph appears above the link to LND's recovery documentation.

Note:
